definition: The JGB swaption vol / USD/JPY vol divergence is a compelling cross-asset relative value opportunity: JGB swaption vol prices BoJ uncertainty while USD/JPY vol is suppressed by carry flows. The trade construction (long USD/JPY risk reversals funded by short JGB swaption vol) provides bounded loss in the dovish scenario and convex upside in the hawkish scenario. August 2024 provides out-of-sample validation.
evidence: Debate pair_0: 'concrete, has a clear mechanism, an out-of-sample validation (August 2024), and asymmetric payoff'
Information-theoretic argument: one vol market is mispricing BoJ risk
Carry flows structurally suppress FX gamma demand, independently supported by vol-regime literature
open_questions: How long the divergence can persist given carry flows as a powerful vol suppression mechanism
Specific options pricing requires live market levels to validate trade construction
Whether BoJ's 30-year track record of disappointing hawks applies to the current cycle
